title: The rise in RAM prices causes a historic crisis in the technology sector
summary: Manufacturers warn of 'RAMageddon', while Apple looks for alternatives in China and DRAM prices skyrocket 50% by 2026.

title: Sony removes hundreds of movies from PlayStation accounts without compensation and the PS6 will cost $1,000
summary: Users lose iconic titles without reimbursement, while the manufacturing cost of the new console skyrockets due to component inflation.

title: Productivity guides, deals and news for the tech consumer
summary: From merging PDFs to setting up TPM, including Movistar car subscriptions and mouse pads, everything the user needs to know.

title: SteamOS expands with new pre-built consoles, Chinese clones and support on Nintendo Switch
summary: Valve and the community boost the ecosystem with official Steam Machines, reverse engineering and a mod that brings the interface to the Switch.

title: Artificial intelligence dominates the agenda amid geopolitical race and citizen distrust
summary: The West accelerates against China, Mistral and Microsoft executives warn Europe, and only 16% of Americans trust AI.

title: Volkswagen, Ford and Toyota undertake the biggest transformation of the automotive industry
summary: VW prepares massive layoffs and plant closures, Ford rehires human engineers and Toyota defends evolution against Chinese pressure.

title: New horizons in PC hardware: Chinese processors, Zen 6 and DDR6
summary: China launches its Loongson CPU, AMD prepares its Zen 6 cores, record DDR6 memory is expected and Japan creates toy PC parts.

title: Emulation and retrocomputing advance: PS4 without PSN on PC and Windows 11 on DDR1
summary: The ShadPS4 emulator achieves multiplayer without official servers, an enthusiast runs Crysis on 2003 hardware and the Radeon RX 480 turns 10 years old.

title: The best deals of the week: Windows 11 LTSC, PNY RAM and MediaMarkt
summary: Hardware and software enthusiasts can take advantage of discounts on DDR5 memory, Windows licenses and a special selection from MediaMarkt.

title: GeForce RTX 5090 suffers new episodes of combustion in its 16-pin connector
summary: Several users report completely burned GPUs and manufacturers reject warranty claims for catastrophic damage.

title: SoftBank defends AI from bubble criticism and questions Musk's orbital centers
summary: Masayoshi Son calls criticism of AI 'blasphemy', while doubts arise about the viability of Elon Musk's space data centers.

title: Claude Code and OpenAI Codex present vulnerabilities that allow attackers to exploit them
summary: Mozilla demonstrates that AI agents for programming can be tricked into installing malware and fail to exclude sensitive files.

title: United States, Europe and Australia push a wave of regulation on privacy and technology
summary: Laws on surveillance, labor rights and digital security are tightened in California, the European Union and Australia.

title: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan faces conflict of interest over Qualcomm purchase
summary: He will receive Qualcomm shares after the acquisition of a startup in which he had invested, raising doubts about ethics on the board.

title: 400 domains illegally streaming the 2026 World Cup seized
summary: The US Department of Justice quintuples the scale of previous operations against sports streaming piracy.

title: Hacker News community shares projects, history and software
summary: From a Bash script to create blogs to the Japanese Daisugi technique, through reverse engineering of Space Shuttle circuits.

title: Spain and Europe face the climate and energy crisis with renewables and fight against ecological disaster
summary: The end of Russian gas accelerates renewables, warming advances relentlessly, the Granada dam wastes water, eucalyptus trees are a green desert and space lettuce gets sick.

title: Mysteries of history and culture: sunken submarines, Chernobyl and Roman enigmas
summary: Eighty years later a sunken ship is discovered instead of a submarine, a hole in Chernobyl is closed and an Aragonese site keeps secrets.

title: Consumer technology is renewed: smart ovens, high-end tablets
summary: The analysis of the Huawei MatePad Pro Max confirms it as a reference tablet, the LG oven conquers the kitchen and luxury arrives with a cryogenic yacht.

title: Startups, business and consumer technology mark the business week
summary: Startup scouts see a market full of liquidity, Foxconn expands its empire, TechCrunch analyzes Tesla's FSD and a writer advocates for dematerialization.
